data.title: Google to deploy AI to speed up connections at largest U.S. power grid operator
data.description:
PJM Interconnection, North America's largest electrical grid operator, announced a multiyear  collaboration Thursday with Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) Google unit to roll out artificial intelligence technologies aimed at getting new power supplies connected faster, marking the first time AI would be used to comprehensively manage an interconnection queue.

Wait times have grown to historic lengths in recent years for connecting grids to new electricity supplies generated from sources like wind, solar and natural gas, worsening an electricity supply crunch in many parts of the U.S., raising power bills and adding to the risk of blackouts, as old power plants retire faster than new supplies can connect.

Google (GOOOG) (GOOGL), in partnership with Alphabet-backed Tapestry, said it will seek to eliminate at least some of the wait by using AI to synthesize information and automate parts of the review and planning processes for projects in line to connect to PJM.

The project is "really about automating a lot of the things that are being laboriously reviewed," Tapestry General Manger Page Crahan told Reuters; the company will work with PJM to develop AI tools and models that will be rolled out in 2025 and phased in over the course of several years.

Over time, the collaboration will develop a model of the PJM grid like a Google Maps for grid information, "bringing in different layers that planners might need to see in a single toggle on and off view to guide faster decisions, introduce new insights, hopefully find efficiencies in those ways," Crahan said.

PJM controls the electrical system that covers 67M people in the eastern U.S., with a territory that includes northern Virginia, the world's largest data center hub.

ETF: (XLU)

data.title: EIA slashes oil price outlook, warns of lower oil demand from tariff uncertainty
data.description:
Crude oil futures fell sharply Thursday, surrendering most of the previous session's strong gains, as the accelerating trade war between the U.S. and China heightens concerns about weaker global demand for energy at the same time that OPEC and its allies raise production.

The WTI and Brent benchmarks surged more than 4% in the previous session after dropping to their lowest levels in more than four years, after President Trump's decision to pause some tariffs against several countries for 90 days, but he also raised duties on China to 125%.

The ongoing trade war between the world's two largest economies is bearish for the global economy, and likely to preclude a large rally in oil, TP ICAP's Scott Shelton said, adding the 90-day pause is "a long time of waiting to see what happens with the rest of the world on tariffs, and short enough to keep people from investing in just about anything if their businesses are correlated with the import/export market."

"With a lot of uncertainty still existing, the prospect for a major rebound in crude is not possible at this stage when the market has to deal with the risk of weakening demand and rising production from OPEC," Saxo Bank's head of commodities strategy Ole Hansen wrote, adding that the world is "still looking at the worst tariffs since the 1930s."

U.S. crude inventories rose by a larger than expected 2.6M barrels last week, government data showed Wednesday, and Macquarie analysts said Thursday they expect another build this week.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery closed -3.6% to $60.07/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June ended -3.3% to $63.33/bbl, this year's second-lowest settlement value for both benchmarks and the fifth day of losses out of six.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) also fell as risk-off sentiment returned to markets, while a bearish 57B cf injection into storage for last week that cut the deficit over the five-year average to just 40B cf was partly offset by record flows of feedgas to LNG export terminals, Gelber & Associates said; the May front-month Nymex contract closed -6.8% to $3.557/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

In a bearish outlook, the U.S. Energy Administration cut its forecast for global oil-demand growth and prices this year, citing an uncertain economic outlook caused by the ongoing trade war.

The agency now expects U.S. WTI crude will drop nearly $7/bbl lower this year with global demand nearly 500K bbl/day lower than previously expected, the EIA said its Short-Term Energy Outlook.

WTI at Cushing, Oklahoma, is now expected to average $63.88/bbl in 2025, the EIA said, $6.80/bbl lower than its March forecast, and will decline further to $57.48/bbl in 2026, $7.49/bbl below the previous outlook, while issuing similar downside revisions to its Brent crude outlook, now forecasting $67.68/bbl in 2025 and $61.48/bbl in 2026.

EIA also guided global consumption of oil and liquid fuels to average 103.64M bbl/day in 2025, down by 490K bbl/day from its prior forecast, and sees consumption in 2026 at 104.68M bbl/day, lower by 620K bbl/day.

U.S. sanctions on Russia, Iran and Venezuela also "create additional uncertainty for crude oil prices," the EIA said.

data.title: Scotts Miracle-Gro cuts cannabis ties, separates Hawthorne Collective to unnamed partner
data.description:
Scotts Miracle-Gro (NYSE:SMG) +2.7% post-market Thursday after saying it is transferring its wholly-owned Hawthorne Collective subsidiary to an undisclosed independent strategic partner, marking the first step in separating its cannabis-related businesses from its core lawn and garden operations.

In exchange for the transfer, Scotts (NYSE:SMG) said it received an interest-bearing promissory note and will retain an option to reacquire the company if cannabis becomes legal at the federal level, or if other measures positively affect the industry.

The move "will reduce the impact of the cannabis sector's volatility on our company's stock and provide opportunities to drive meaningful and immediate value creation through increased investments in our consumer business," Chairman and CEO Jim Hagedorn said, adding that Scotts (SMG) also will look to separate from its Hawthorne Gardening subsidiary by the end of FY 2025.

"The Hawthorne companies were intended to capitalize on the legal cannabis sector, but the ability to achieve sustained growth within this industry has been challenged by four years of unkept promises resulting in total inaction at the federal level on cannabis-related issues," Hagedorn said.

data.title: Gold rallies to new record, miners hit multiyear highs, as tariff outlook remains cloudy
data.description:
Gold futures surged more than 3% for the second straight day to reach an all-time record on Thursday, while several gold mining stocks hit new 52-week highs, as the escalating trade war between the U.S. and China sparked another investor rush into the safe-haven metal.

Gold prices have surged 6.9% over the last three sessions for the largest three-day gain since March 2020, according to Dow Jones.

President Trump said on Wednesday he would begin a 90-day pause on new tariffs for most countries but he also hiked duties on imports from China to 125%, after China had retaliated against Trump's initial with an 84% tariff - moves that are exacerbating concerns that the world's two biggest economies are become entangled in a crippling trade war with no resolution in sight.

"It has been an extraordinary week defined by confusion over Trump's tariff play," which has prompted investors to make a rush to gold, FXTM manager of market analysis Lukman Otunuga said.

Uncertainty remains high in markets, stemming from expectations the U.S.-China trade war will worsen and cause pain for both economies, Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital Securities said.

Meanwhile, the dollar index fell than 1% against rival currencies, making gold less expensive for other currency holders, and new data showed U.S. consumer prices unexpectedly fell in March, but inflation risks are still seen tilting to the upside after Trump doubled down on the China tariffs.

Following the data, traders bet the U.S. Federal Reserve will resume cutting interest rates in June and perhaps reduce its policy rate by a full percentage point by the end of the year.

"We see central banks buying [gold], so as long as we see inflows into ETFs and more of the monetary policy risks, there's a lot of key drivers that will continue to support gold," Allegiance Gold chief operating officer Alex Ebkarian said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery ended +3.2% to an all-time high settlement of $3,155.20/oz, while front-month April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) rose for the fourth straight session, +1.1% to $30.671/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Several precious metals mining stocks tallied new 52-week highs in Thursday's trading, including Harmong Gold (HMY) +10.5%, Gold Fields (GFI) +8.5%, Osisko Gold Royalties (OR) +6.8%, AngloGold Ashanti (AU) +6.6%, Orla Mining (ORLA) +6.4%, Iamgold (IAG) +5.7%, Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM) +5.4%, Alamos Gold (AGI) +5.4%, Kinross Gold (KGC) +5.3%, Aris Mining (ARMN) +5.3%, Royal Gold (RGLD) +3.9%.

Newmont (NEM) did not hit a new high but was the S&P 500's top gainer for the day, finishing +4.5%.

data.title: Retractable Technologies reduces workforce by about 7%
data.description:
• Retractable Technologies (NYSE:RVP) said on Thursday that it has reduced its workforce by approximately 7%. 
  • The reduction is expected to save an estimated $1.6M in annual wages and benefits, or approximately 7.8% of total estimated workforce costs. The expected savings is offset by one-time separation payments of approximately $300 thousand to the affected workers. 
  • Approximately 72% of the targeted payroll reduction affects general and administrative functions, with the remainder coming from manufacturing or manufacturing support positions.
  • The move comes as the company continues to allocate more of its resources to increase its U.S. manufacturing capabilities and reduce reliance on products produced in China. 
  • While contract manufacturers in China have historically produced most of the products the company sells, the material financial impact of tariffs on imports from China eliminates the company’s ability to continue the practice. 
  • As a result, the company will work to minimize its exposure to the tariffs and strategically import only those products which it cannot make domestically and rely on its Little Elm facility to produce the majority of its products.

data.title: Pure cycle projects $30M revenue target for FY2025 driven by Sky Ranch development
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pure Cycle Corporation (PCYO) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Mark Harding reported $4 million in revenue for Q2 2025, with a gross margin of 38%, largely driven by royalty income. He emphasized strong performance in mineral royalties and noted that the company is progressing well toward its fiscal 2025 revenue guidance of $30–31 million.
  • Harding highlighted the ongoing development at the Sky Ranch community, with three phases of construction underway and a new Phase 2e added, increasing development capacity. He noted the demand for entry-level homes in the Denver area and confirmed builder confidence with continued lot deliveries.
  • CFO Marc Spezialy noted solid financial performance across all segments, including land development, water utilities, and single-family rentals. He also emphasized the high profit margins from royalty income and recurring revenues.

Outlook

  • Harding reiterated the fiscal 2025 revenue guidance of approximately $30–31 million, with stronger performance expected in Q3 and Q4 due to seasonality and lot delivery sequencing.
  • The company plans to maintain momentum by delivering additional lots in Phases 2c and 2d of Sky Ranch and initiating Phase 2e development.
  • Management expects continued growth in recurring revenues from water utilities and single-family rentals.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q2 2025 was $4 million, with a year-to-date total of $10 million, reflecting progress toward the annual target. Gross profit margins remained robust, exceeding 50% year-to-date.
  • Earnings per share (EPS) stood at $0.20 for the first six months, supported by consistent net income generation.
  • The Water Utilities segment was bolstered by tap fee revenues, while oil and gas deliveries were softer but expected to improve in fiscal 2026.
  • The company reported strong demand for rentals, maintaining high occupancy rates and favorable margins in the single-family rental segment.

Q&A

  • Bill Miller, Private Investor: Questioned the timeline for the I-70 interchange project and potential land acquisitions. CEO Harding explained that the interchange permit is expected by the end of 2025, with construction starting in 2026. He noted increased seller interest in land acquisitions but emphasized the priority of accelerating Sky Ranch development.
  • Nigel, Analyst: Asked about builder sentiment and rental demand. Harding highlighted strong builder confidence with ongoing spec home construction and noted a temporary gap in rental inventory due to permitting delays but expected stronger activity in the coming quarters.
  • Geoffrey Scott, Scott Asset Management: Inquired about the relationship between the new interchange and commercial development. Harding confirmed that commercial opportunities would increase with the interchange completion, expected by 2026.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on project timelines and market demand. Their tone indicated interest in long-term growth opportunities but also highlighted concerns about timelines for infrastructure and land acquisitions.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and responses, emphasizing strategic progress and market positioning. Harding used phrases like "we are on track" and "strong performance" to convey confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained steady, with management continuing to project confidence in achieving guidance and addressing challenges in a measured manner.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue in Q2 2025 was seasonally lower than Q1 2025 due to the construction slowdown during winter months, a trend consistent with prior years.
  • The previous quarter saw higher gross margins at 64%, attributed to strong oil and gas royalty income, compared to 38% in Q2.
  • Management's focus on recurring revenues and expanding development phases remained consistent.
  • Analysts in Q2 raised more questions about specific project timelines and market dynamics, reflecting increased scrutiny on execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted weaker oil and gas deliveries in Q2 but highlighted a strong pipeline of well permits for fiscal 2026 and beyond.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about potential delays in infrastructure projects like the I-70 interchange and their impact on commercial development.
  • The company acknowledged market challenges, including rising interest rates affecting homebuyer affordability, but emphasized its competitive positioning in the entry-level housing segment.

Final Takeaway

Pure Cycle Corporation continues to execute its strategic plan with significant progress in the Sky Ranch development, maintaining strong financial performance and advancing its fiscal 2025 revenue target of $30–31 million. The company remains focused on leveraging its competitive position in the Denver housing market, expanding recurring revenue streams, and addressing infrastructure and development challenges to drive long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Centene subsidiary awarded Nevada medicaid contract
data.description:
• Centene (NYSE:CNC) said on Thursday that its Nevada subsidiary, SilverSummit Healthplan has been selected by the Nevada Department of Health and Human Services to provide services for its Medicaid managed care program. 
  • Subject to state approval, the five-year contract is expected to begin January 1, 2026, with a possible two-year extension. 
  • SilverSummit Healthplan has served Nevada Medicaid members since 2017 and currently serves over 100,000 members. Starting January 1, 2026, SilverSummit will continue to serve Medicaid members providing managed care services including primary care, telehealth, pharmacy, maternal and behavioral health. 
  • Ambetter from SilverSummit HealthPlan, SilverSummit's marketplace offering, was also one of three bidders selected by the Nevada Department of Health and Human Services for its marketplace public option, the Battle Born State Plan (BBSP). Effective January 1, 2026, the five-year contract includes a potential two-year extension and is expected to cover 16,000 individuals in the first year. 
  • Centene (NYSE:CNC) shares were up 0.56% in extended trading.

data.title: Paranovus Entertainment receives Nasdaq letter over non-compliance with listing rules
data.description:
• Paranovus Entertainment Technology (NASDAQ:PAVS) received a letter from the Nasdaq Stock Market on April 9 notifying that the company is not in compliance with the requirements for continued listing because it did not timely file its Form 6-K for the period ended September 30, 2024.
  • The company has 60 calendar days from the date of the deficiency letter to submit a plan to regain compliance with Nasdaq's listing rules.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NewHold Investment announces separate trading of its ordinary shares and warrants
data.description:
• NewHold Investment Corp III (NASDAQ:NHIC) said on Thursday that commencing April 17, 2025, holders of the units sold in the company's initial public offering completed on March 3, 2025, may elect to separately trade the class A ordinary shares of the company and the warrants included in such units on Nasdaq. 
  • The ordinary shares and warrants that are separated will trade on Nasdaq under the symbols “NHIC” and “NHICW,” respectively. Those units not separated will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol “NHICU.”

data.title: House approves Trump's 'big' budget framework, after swaying GOP holdouts
data.description:
House Republicans approved a budget framework on Thursday after House Speaker Mike Johnson was able to convince GOP holdouts that he would seek at least $1.5T in cuts to federal programs and services. Johnson achieved the win after he halted voting on Wednesday night to hammer out a deal.

The bill includes trillions of dollars in tax breaks without the deeper cuts the holdouts were seeking, the Associated Press reported. The 216-214 vote advanced the budget plan. Two conservative Republicans, along with all Democrats, voted against it.

The next step is turning the framework into bill text. If more than a couple Republicans vote against the plan at any step in the process, the plan can come to a halt. While the Democrats are in the minority, they promised to object at every turn.

House Democratic leader Hakeem Jeffries called the Republican budget plan a "toxic scheme' that proposed the biggest cuts to Medicaid and food assistance in U.S. history, "all in service of enacting massive tax breaks to their millionaire donors, like Elon Musk" — a reference to the Tesla chairman's Department of Government Efficiency cost-cutting campaign.

Johnson was able to advance the plan after a private meeting with a group of Republicans on Wednesday night worked to discuss their options.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
